message:
feat(session-v2): add resilient index auto-recovery on create and improved segment enumeration

Make SessionStoreV2::create() self-healing when the index file is missing,
corrupt, or out of sync with segment data, rather than propagating errors
that leave sessions appearing empty.

Recovery logic in create():
- Catch bootstrap_from_disk() errors and classify via new
  is_recoverable_index_error() — matches checksum mismatch, index out of
  bounds, index/frame mismatch, payload integrity mismatch, sequence
  ordering violations, and JSON parse errors
- When recoverable: log warning, call rebuild_index(), re-bootstrap
- When entry_count is 0 but segments_exist_with_data(): rebuild index
  proactively (handles missing/empty index file case)
- Post-recovery validate_integrity() with same recover-rebuild-revalidate
  fallback chain

New helpers:
- list_segment_files(): enumerate *.seg files sorted by sequence number,
  replacing the manual sequential-probe loop in rebuild_index()
- segments_exist_with_data(): quick check for non-empty segment files
- is_recoverable_index_error(): classify errors as index-only (recoverable)
  vs segment/data (not recoverable)

segment_count fix:
- Use BTreeSet distinct count instead of max(segment_seq) which could
  over-count when segment sequence numbers have gaps

Tests:
- create_recovers_when_index_file_is_missing_but_segments_exist: delete
  index file, reopen, verify all 4 entries recovered with integrity
- create_recovers_when_index_json_is_corrupt: write garbage JSON to index,
  reopen, verify all 5 entries recovered with integrity
- Renamed migration_status_corrupt_when_index_damaged →
  migration_status_self_heals_when_index_damaged: index corruption is now
  auto-recovered, so status returns Migrated instead of Corrupt
- Updated e2e_corrupt_migration_recovery_cleanup_only: corrupt segment data
  (not just index) to test truly unrecoverable corruption path

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
